trending_themes.theme: ARIRANG comeback after full military discharge
trending_themes.evidence: ARIRANG album released March 20, 2026 with 4M first-day sales and 110M Spotify streams in 24 hours — K-pop record. All seven members discharged by June 2025.
Won Artist of the Year at 2026 American Music Awards on May 25. All 7 members achieved Hot 100 chart positions simultaneously.

trending_themes.theme: ARIRANG World Tour — largest stadium tour in K-pop history
trending_themes.evidence: 82+ dates through March 2027. Four sold-out nights at Allegiant Stadium Las Vegas.

trending_themes.theme: Mixed critical reception despite commercial dominance
trending_themes.evidence: ARIRANG scores ranged from 90 (AllMusic) to 53 (Pitchfork) to 30 (Needle Drop). Experimental direction drew debate.

notable_movements: Jimin's 'Who' — 2.4B streams, first solo K-pop w/o feature at milestone

annotation_hooks: Military service as thematic throughline — BTS lyrics about identity and sacrifice now deeply personal as all members return from duty; 'ARIRANG' (Korean folk anthem) as album title symbolizes homecoming and national identity
Member interdependence showcased through solo success — individual Hot 100 placements prove autonomous artistry, making group return feel like chosen reunion not obligation
Post-hiatus vulnerability: critical split (90→30) reflects discourse on whether groups maintain relevance after extended military breaks
raw_headlines: BTS Takes Over Las Vegas After Military Reunions
BTS Wins Artist of the Year at 2026 AMAs
ARIRANG Breaks Spotify K-pop Record — 110M Streams in 24 Hours
All Seven BTS Members Now on Billboard Hot 100 Simultaneously
Limited Edition Hotteok Oreos with BTS
